summary: PR #2330 matched the remaining function in ftKb_SpecialNPk by changing local Fighter pointer handling and stack padding in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim. The automated report says the unit main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk gained one new match: ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim from 97.36% to 100.00%, contributing +456 matched bytes overall.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; updated ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim to reach a reported 100.00% match.

what_changed.text: Added extra Fighter pointer locals fp2 and fp3 in ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim, including fp3 = (fp2 = fp), while retaining fp initialized with GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added Fighter* fp3; Fighter* fp2 = GET_FIGHTER(gobj); fp3 = (fp2 = fp);

what_changed.text: Changed PAD_STACK from decimal 28 to PAD_STACK(0x18).
Changed the later charge counter and cancel-path accesses from fp to fp3 for mv.kb.specialhi.x4, fv.kb.xA8, and the ftCo_800BFFD0 argument.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fp->mv/fv accesses replaced with fp3->mv/fv and ftCo_800BFFD0(fp3, 0x36, 0)

what_changed.text: PR body says the author wanted to also link but submitted only this because linking seemed hard for a split file from ftkb_init.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Wanted to also link, but that seems hard since it's a split file from ftkb_init

smart_moves.text: Kept the PR narrowly scoped to one function in one source file rather than mixing the match with broader link/split work that the author described as difficult.
Used narrow local pointer lifetime/register-shape changes rather than changing the function's external behavior or interfaces.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local fp2/fp3 additions and access substitutions only within ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim

decomp_lessons.text: For near-matching fighter functions, preserving multiple local Fighter pointer names that alias the same object can be a necessary codegen tactic, but this is a matching tactic rather than a general style preference.

PAD_STACK size adjustments should be interpreted as narrow match steering and kept tied to verifier evidence; here the bot report supports that the final function matched after PAD_STACK and local-lifetime changes.

naming_conventions.text: The PR used simple numbered local aliases fp2 and fp3 for Fighter pointers where the role was codegen/lifetime steering rather than a newly proven semantic concept.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Duplicate GET_FIGHTER(gobj) into fp2 and then assign fp3 = (fp2 = fp), apparently to affect local lifetime/register or stack shape for the match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Fighter* fp2 = GET_FIGHTER(gobj); fp3 = (fp2 = fp);

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Use fp3, not fp, for the later repeated field updates and ftCo_800BFFD0 call in the charge/cancel path.
Adjust PAD_STACK to PAD_STACK(0x18) in the matched function.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code 58.98% (+0.01%, +456 bytes) and one new match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNPk ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim, +13 bytes, 97.36% before and 100.00% after.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Fighter pointer aliasing plus PAD_STACK closed ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2330, ftKb_SsSpecialNHold_Anim reached a reported 100.00% match after adding Fighter pointer aliases fp2/fp3, assigning fp3 = (fp2 = fp), using fp3 for later charge/cancel-path accesses, and changing PAD_STACK to 0x18. Treat this as a narrow matching tactic with verifier evidence, not as a general style rule.
